title: A professional athlete or coach for a foreign team doesn’t need a work permit - Canada.ca
content:
## To work in Canada as an athlete or a coach
FIFA-invited workers and volunteers
You don’t need a work permit to work in Canada if you’ve been directly invited to work at the FIFA World Cup 26™.
You may need to apply for a visitor visa or an electronic travel authorization (eTA) .
You don’t need a work permit if 1 of the following applies to you:
- you’re a foreign athlete or coach
- you’re a member of a foreign team competing in Canada
- you’re a member of a Canadian amateur team

## If you’ll work for a Canadian professional team
You do need a work permit.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
